creator: alexander@wittig.name
text:
I agree with ALT_CONFIG_ROOT_ONLY, it is now obsolete. Also glad to hear 
that WITHOUT_D_OPTION will make it into the port.

About ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX:
It is my understanding from 
http://www.exim.org/exim-html-current/doc/html/spec_html/ch52.html that
(a) if it is not set at all in the EDITME file, ANY config file can be 
specified via -C
(b) if it is set to some string (the port option is checked, which sets 
it to PREFIX/etc/exim), ONLY config files starting with that string can 
be specified via -C
I believe this is independent from the question what user privileges 
(root, exim, ...) the final exim process retains.
In my tests with 4.72 the above description is also the way the final 
exim binaries behaved.

In the Makefile, the test is

.if defined(WITHOUT_ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX)
SEDLIST+=	-e 's,^(ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX=),\# \1,'
.endif

and the line in EDITME (patched by the port patch) is

ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX=XX_PREFIX_XX/etc/exim/

Running "make patch" once with and once without the option checked gave 
me the following results: When the options is NOT checked, the 
corresponding line in the EDITME file is commented out, and 
ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX is not set at all (case (a)). When the option is set, 
the line survives (case (b)). So it is my understanding that in order to 
LIMIT the config files allowed, I have to CHECK the option (which is the 
opposite of what the option says). The comment in EDITME describing 
ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX also says "...You can restrict the location of 
alternate configurations by defining a prefix below...", so setting this 
option will restrict something, not allow more.

And about Spamooborona: you're certainly in a better position to 
determine the correct spelling. But it is misspelled in either the file 
"options" (Spamoborona) or "Makefile.options" (Spamooborona) ;-)

Alex


> From what I see in ftp://exim.inode.at/exim/ChangeLogs/NewStuff-4.73
>
> - ALT_CONFIG_ROOT_ONLY is now forced to 1, so we don't need
> the respective hunks for 4.73;
>
> - WITHOUT_D_OPTION is good and I'll include it to the updated
> port for 4.73;
>
>
> Also, from http://bugs.exim.org/show_bug.cgi?id=3D1044 I can
> conclude that setting ALT_CONFIG_PREFIX really allows
> other configuration files to be used _and_ root privileges
> to be retained. But there is also TRUSTED_CONFIG_LIST
> variable and it creates some uncertainty for me. I'll dig
> up the sources of 4.73 to understand how to proceed.
>
>
> Other points:
>
> - "Spamooborona" is spelled correctly ;))
>
>
> If I had missed something, please, feel free to correct me.
> ------------------------------------------------------------------------
> Eygene Ryabinkin ,,,^..^,,,
